target_meaning: a loner, a person isolated or excluded from the rest of the group

source_meaning: alternative form of رَجَعَ بِخُفَّيْ حُنَيْنٍ (rajaʕa biḵuffay ḥunaynin)
target_meaning: to be at the back of one's mind; to keep coming to mind

source_meaning: to the nines, to the hilt, at its best, at its finest
target_meaning: to find oneself in a suitable environment where one can perform well, up someone's alley, in one's element
adverbial form of 시(時)도 때도 없다 (sido ttaedo eopda, “to be recurrent”): recurrently

source_meaning: As you sow, so shall you reap.
target_meaning: something that happens to someone who is thought to deserve it; something that serves someone right

source_meaning: synonym of اِنْقَلَبَ عَلَى عَقِبَيْهِ (inqalaba ʕalā ʕaqibayhi)
target_meaning: synonym of 인의 장막 (in-ui jangmak, “all senses”)

source_meaning: to die, to pass away, to decease
target_meaning: to fall asleep, go to sleep, nod off
to try and fall asleep; to lull oneself to sleep

source_meaning: the matter has become unbearable, reached its limit; enough is enough
target_meaning: enough with, out with, it's over for (Used to refer to something which has just become obsolete or been done away with due to the introduction of something newer or better)
get blood out of a stone (something that is almost impossible)
